Employees.question: Return all employees with employeeid, firstname, lastname, and email, ordered by lastname then firstname.
Employees.answer: SELECT employeeid, firstname, lastname, email FROM employees ORDER BY lastname ASC, firstname ASC;

Employees.question: Return firstname, lastname, salary for employees with salary greater than 80000. Order by salary descending.
Employees.answer: SELECT firstname, lastname, salary FROM employees WHERE salary > 80000 ORDER BY salary DESC;

Employees.question: Return the total number of employees as a single value named employeecount.
Employees.answer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS employeecount FROM employees;

Employees.question: Return firstname, lastname, departmentname, and location for all employees who have a department assigned.
Employees.answer: SELECT e.firstname, e.lastname, d.departmentname, d.location FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON d.departmentid = e.departmentid;

Employees.question: Return departmentname and average salary (alias avgsalary) for each department. Only include departments with average salary greater than 70000. Order by avgsalary descending.
Employees.answer: SELECT d.departmentname, AVG(e.salary) AS avgsalary FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON d.departmentid = e.departmentid GROUP BY d.departmentid, d.departmentname HAVING AVG(e.salary) > 70000 ORDER BY avgsalary DESC;

Employees.question: Return employees who do not have a matching department record. Show employeeid, firstname, lastname.
Employees.answer: SELECT e.employeeid, e.firstname, e.lastname FROM employees e LEFT JOIN departments d ON d.departmentid = e.departmentid WHERE d.departmentid IS NULL;

Employees.question: Return employees whose salary is greater than the average salary of their own department. Show firstname, lastname, salary, departmentid.
Employees.answer: SELECT e.firstname, e.lastname, e.salary, e.departmentid FROM employees e WHERE e.departmentid IS NOT NULL AND e.salary > (SELECT AVG(e2.salary) FROM employees e2 WHERE e2.departmentid = e.departmentid);

Employees.question: Return departments where total salary expense exceeds the department budget. Show departmentname, budget, totalsalary.
Employees.answer: SELECT d.departmentname, d.budget, SUM(e.salary) AS totalsalary FROM departments d INNER JOIN employees e ON e.departmentid = d.departmentid GROUP BY d.departmentid, d.departmentname, d.budget HAVING SUM(e.salary) > d.budget;

Employees.question: Return the top 2 highest-paid employees in each department. Show departmentid, firstname, lastname, salary. Include ties for the 2nd position.
Employees.answer: SELECT departmentid, firstname, lastname, salary FROM (SELECT e.departmentid, e.firstname, e.lastname, e.salary, DENSE_RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY e.departmentid ORDER BY e.salary DESC) AS dr FROM employees e WHERE e.departmentid IS NOT NULL) ranked WHERE dr <= 2 ORDER BY departmentid ASC, salary DESC;

Employees.question: Select all employees.
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM employees;

Employees.question: Get employee first and last names.
Employees.answer: SELECT firstname, lastname FROM employees;

Employees.question: Count total employees.
Employees.answer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM employees;

Employees.question: Get all unique department IDs from employees.
Employees.answer: SELECT DISTINCT departmentid FROM employees WHERE departmentid IS NOT NULL;

Employees.question: Get employees ordered by last name.
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY lastname ASC;

Employees.question: Get top 5 highest paid employees.
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY salary DESC LIMIT 5;

Employees.question: Get employees with salary greater than 80000.
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE salary > 80000;

Employees.question: Get employees in department 3.
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE departmentid = 3;

Employees.question: Get average employee salary.
Employees.answer: SELECT AVG(salary) AS avgsalary FROM employees;

Employees.question: Get employees whose first name starts with A.
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE firstname LIKE 'A%';

Employees.question: Get the highest salary in the company.
Employees.answer: SELECT MAX(salary) AS maxsalary FROM employees;

Employees.question: Get the lowest salary in the company.
Employees.answer: SELECT MIN(salary) AS minsalary FROM employees;

Employees.question: Get total salary expense.
Employees.answer: SELECT SUM(salary) AS totalsalary FROM employees;

Employees.question: Get number of employees per department (excluding departments with no employees).
Employees.answer: SELECT departmentid, COUNT(employeeid) AS total FROM employees WHERE departmentid IS NOT NULL GROUP BY departmentid;

Employees.question: Get employees with email containing 'company'.
Employees.answer: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE email LIKE '%company%';

Departments.question: Get department names and locations ordered by departmentname.
Departments.answer: SELECT departmentname, location FROM departments ORDER BY departmentname ASC;

Departments.question: Count total departments as departmentcount.
Departments.answer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS departmentcount FROM departments;

Departments.question: Get departments ordered by name.
Departments.answer: SELECT * FROM departments ORDER BY departmentname ASC;

Departments.question: Get departments with no employees.
Departments.answer: SELECT d.* FROM departments d LEFT JOIN employees e ON d.departmentid = e.departmentid WHERE e.departmentid IS NULL;

Departments.question: Get employees with their department name.
Departments.answer: SELECT e.firstname, e.lastname, d.departmentname FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON e.departmentid = d.departmentid;

Departments.question: Get average salary per department.
Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentname, AVG(e.salary) AS avg_salary FROM employees e INNER JOIN departments d ON e.departmentid = d.departmentid GROUP BY d.departmentname;

Departments.question: Get departments with more than 3 employees.
Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentname, COUNT(e.employeeid) AS total FROM departments d INNER JOIN employees e ON d.departmentid = e.departmentid GROUP BY d.departmentname HAVING COUNT(e.employeeid) > 3;

Departments.question: Get the department with the highest total salary.
Departments.answer: SELECT d.departmentname, SUM(e.salary) AS total FROM departments d INNER JOIN employees e ON d.departmentid = e.departmentid GROUP BY d.departmentname ORDER BY total DESC LIMIT 1;

Customers.question: Return customerid, firstname, lastname, city for customers in Canada, ordered by lastname then firstname.
Customers.answer: SELECT customerid, firstname, lastname, city FROM customers WHERE LOWER(country) = 'canada' ORDER BY lastname ASC, firstname ASC;

Customers.question: Return the total number of orders as ordercount.
Customers.answer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS ordercount FROM orders;

Customers.question: Return orderid, orderdate, totalamount for orders with totalamount >= 1000, ordered by orderdate descending.
Customers.answer: SELECT orderid, orderdate, totalamount FROM orders WHERE totalamount >= 1000 ORDER BY orderdate DESC;

Customers.question: Return orderid, orderdate, customer full name as customername, and totalamount for all orders.
Customers.answer: SELECT o.orderid, o.orderdate, c.firstname || ' ' || c.lastname AS customername, o.totalamount FROM orders o INNER JOIN customers c ON c.customerid = o.customerid;

Customers.question: Return customerid and total spend (alias totalspent) for each customer with totalspent > 2000. Order by totalspent descending.
Customers.answer: SELECT c.customerid, SUM(o.totalamount) AS totalspent FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON o.customerid = c.customerid GROUP BY c.customerid HAVING SUM(o.totalamount) > 2000 ORDER BY totalspent DESC;

Customers.question: Return city and number of orders (alias ordersincity) for each city, ordered by ordersincity descending.
Customers.answer: SELECT c.city, COUNT(o.orderid) AS ordersincity FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON o.customerid = c.customerid GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY ordersincity DESC;

Customers.question: Return each customerid with their most recent orderdate and totalamount. Include all rows matching the most recent date.
Customers.answer: SELECT o.customerid, o.orderdate, o.totalamount FROM orders o WHERE o.orderdate = (SELECT MAX(o2.orderdate) FROM orders o2 WHERE o2.customerid = o.customerid);

Customers.question: Return customers who have never placed an order. Show customerid, firstname, lastname.
Customers.answer: SELECT c.customerid, c.firstname, c.lastname FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customerid = c.customerid WHERE o.orderid IS NULL;

Customers.question: Return monthly totals with the previous month total (alias prevtotal) and difference (alias diff). Month format YYYY-MM, ordered by month.
Customers.answer: WITH monthly AS (SELECT SUBSTR(orderdate, 1, 7) AS month, SUM(totalamount) AS total FROM orders GROUP BY SUBSTR(orderdate, 1, 7)) SELECT month, total, LAG(total) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prevtotal, total - LAG(total) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS diff FROM monthly ORDER BY month;

Customers.question: Get customer first and last names.
Count total customers.
Customers.answer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM customers;

Customers.question: Get customers ordered by last name.
Customers.answer: SELECT * FROM customers ORDER BY lastname ASC;

Customers.question: Get distinct countries customers are from.
Get customers whose last name contains 'son'.
Customers.answer: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE lastname LIKE '%son%';

Customers.question: Get customers from a specific city Toronto.
Count customers per country.
Customers.answer: SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS total FROM customers GROUP BY country;

Orders.question: Get total number of orders.
Orders.answer: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM orders;

Orders.question: Get orders ordered by date descending.
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY orderdate DESC;

Orders.question: Get total revenue from all orders.
Orders.answer: SELECT SUM(totalamount) AS total_revenue FROM orders;

Orders.question: Get average order amount.
Orders.answer: SELECT AVG(totalamount) AS avg_amount FROM orders;

Orders.question: Get orders with amount greater than 100.
Get orders placed in 2025.
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE orderdate BETWEEN '2025-01-01' AND '2025-12-31';

Orders.question: Get number of orders per customer.
Orders.answer: SELECT customerid, COUNT(orderid) AS total_orders FROM orders GROUP BY customerid;

Orders.question: Get customers with their orders.
Orders.answer: SELECT c.firstname, c.lastname, o.orderid, o.totalamount FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customerid = o.customerid;

Orders.question: Get customers who have more than 1 order.
Orders.answer: SELECT customerid, COUNT(orderid) AS total FROM orders GROUP BY customerid HAVING COUNT(orderid) > 1;

Orders.question: Get the highest order amount.
Orders.answer: SELECT MAX(totalamount) AS max_amount FROM orders;

Orders.question: Get the customer who spent the most.
Orders.answer: SELECT customerid, SUM(totalamount) AS total_spent FROM orders GROUP BY customerid ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 1;

Orders.question: Get total revenue per customer.
Orders.answer: SELECT c.firstname, c.lastname, SUM(o.totalamount) AS revenue FROM customers c INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customerid = o.customerid GROUP BY c.customerid;

Orders.question: Get orders sorted by amount descending.
Orders.answer: SELECT * FROM orders ORDER BY totalamount DESC;
